1.text: Now the word<S>1697</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> came unto Jonah<S>3124</S> the son<S>1121</S> of Amittai<S>573</S>, saying<S>559</S>, <sup>Jonah: Gr. Jonas</sup>

Arise<S>6965</S>, go<S>3212</S> to Nineveh<S>5210</S>, that great<S>1419</S> city<S>5892</S>, and cry<S>7121</S> against it; for their wickedness<S>7451</S> is come up<S>5927</S> before<S>6440</S> me.

But Jonah<S>3124</S> rose up<S>6965</S> to flee<S>1272</S> unto Tarshish<S>8659</S> from the presence<S>6440</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>, and went down<S>3381</S> to Joppa<S>3305</S>; and he found<S>4672</S> a ship<S>591</S> going<S>935</S> to Tarshish<S>8659</S>: so he paid<S>5414</S> the fare<S>7939</S> thereof, and went down<S>3381</S> into it, to go<S>935</S> with them unto Tarshish<S>8659</S> from the presence<S>6440</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>.

But the LORD<S>3068</S> sent out<S>2904</S> a great<S>1419</S> wind<S>7307</S> into the sea<S>3220</S>, and there was a mighty<S>1419</S> tempest<S>5591</S> in the sea<S>3220</S>, so that the ship<S>591</S> was like<S>2803</S> to be broken<S>7665</S>. <sup>sent out: Heb. cast forth</sup> <sup>was like: Heb. thought to be broken</sup>

Then the mariners<S>4419</S> were afraid<S>3372</S>, and cried<S>2199</S> every man<S>376</S> unto his god<S>430</S>, and cast forth<S>2904</S> the wares<S>3627</S> that were in the ship<S>591</S> into the sea<S>3220</S>, to lighten<S>7043</S> it of them. But Jonah<S>3124</S> was gone down<S>3381</S> into the sides<S>3411</S> of the ship<S>5600</S>; and he lay<S>7901</S>, and was fast asleep<S>7290</S>.

So the shipmaster<S>7227</S> <S>2259</S> came<S>7126</S> to him, and said<S>559</S> unto him, What meanest thou, O sleeper<S>7290</S>? arise<S>6965</S>, call<S>7121</S> upon thy God<S>430</S>, if so be that God<S>430</S> will think<S>6245</S> upon us, that we perish<S>6</S> not.

And they said<S>559</S> every one<S>376</S> to his fellow<S>7453</S>, Come<S>3212</S>, and let us cast<S>5307</S> lots<S>1486</S>, that we may know<S>3045</S> for whose cause<S>7945</S> this evil<S>7451</S> is upon us. So they cast<S>5307</S> lots<S>1486</S>, and the lot<S>1486</S> fell<S>5307</S> upon Jonah<S>3124</S>.

Then said<S>559</S> they unto him, Tell<S>5046</S> us, we pray thee, for whose cause this evil<S>7451</S> is upon us; What is thine occupation<S>4399</S>? and whence<S>370</S> comest<S>935</S> thou? what is thy country<S>776</S>? and of what people<S>5971</S> art thou?

And he said<S>559</S> unto them, I am an Hebrew<S>5680</S>; and I fear<S>3373</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, the God<S>430</S> of heaven<S>8064</S>, which hath made<S>6213</S> the sea<S>3220</S> and the dry<S>3004</S> land. <sup>the LORD: or, JEHOVAH</sup>

Then were the men<S>582</S> exceedingly<S>1419</S> <S>3374</S> afraid<S>3372</S>, and said<S>559</S> unto him, Why hast thou done<S>6213</S> this? For the men<S>582</S> knew<S>3045</S> that he fled<S>1272</S> from the presence<S>6440</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>, because he had told<S>5046</S> them. <sup>exceedingly: Heb. with great fear</sup>

Then said<S>559</S> they unto him, What shall we do<S>6213</S> unto thee, that the sea<S>3220</S> may be calm<S>8367</S> unto us? for the sea<S>3220</S> wrought<S>1980</S>, and was tempestuous<S>5590</S>. <sup>may: Heb. may be silent from us</sup> <sup>wrought: or, grew more and more tempestuous: Heb. went</sup>

And he said<S>559</S> unto them, Take me up<S>5375</S>, and cast me forth<S>2904</S> into the sea<S>3220</S>; so shall the sea<S>3220</S> be calm<S>8367</S> unto you: for I know<S>3045</S> that for my sake<S>7945</S> this great<S>1419</S> tempest<S>5591</S> is upon you.

Nevertheless the men<S>582</S> rowed hard<S>2864</S> to bring<S>7725</S> it to the land<S>3004</S>; but they could<S>3201</S> not: for the sea<S>3220</S> wrought<S>1980</S>, and was tempestuous<S>5590</S> against them. <sup>rowed: Heb. digged</sup> <sup>wrought: or, grew more and more tempestuous: Heb. went</sup>

Wherefore they cried<S>7121</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, and said<S>559</S>, We beseech thee<S>577</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, we beseech thee, let us not perish<S>6</S> for this man's<S>376</S> life<S>5315</S>, and lay<S>5414</S> not upon us innocent<S>5355</S> <S>5355</S> blood<S>1818</S>: for thou, O LORD<S>3068</S>, hast done<S>6213</S> as it pleased<S>2654</S> thee.
So they took up<S>5375</S> Jonah<S>3124</S>, and cast him forth<S>2904</S> into the sea<S>3220</S>: and the sea<S>3220</S> ceased<S>5975</S> from her raging<S>2197</S>. <sup>ceased: Heb. stood</sup>

Then the men<S>582</S> feared<S>3372</S> the LORD<S>3068</S> exceedingly<S>1419</S> <S>3374</S>, and offered<S>2076</S> a sacrifice<S>2077</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, and made<S>5087</S> vows<S>5088</S>. <sup>offered: Heb. sacrifice unto the LORD, and vowed vows</sup>

Now the LORD<S>3068</S> had prepared<S>4487</S> a great<S>1419</S> fish<S>1709</S> to swallow up<S>1104</S> Jonah<S>3124</S>. And Jonah<S>3124</S> was in the belly<S>4578</S> of the fish<S>1709</S> three<S>7969</S> days<S>3117</S> and three<S>7969</S> nights<S>3915</S>. <sup>belly: Heb. bowels</sup>

2.text: Then Jonah<S>3124</S> prayed<S>6419</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S> his God<S>430</S> out of the fish's<S>1710</S> belly<S>4578</S>,

And said<S>559</S>, I cried<S>7121</S> by reason of mine affliction<S>6869</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, and he heard<S>6030</S> me; out of the belly<S>990</S> of hell<S>7585</S> cried<S>7768</S> I, and thou heardest<S>8085</S> my voice<S>6963</S>. <sup>by: or, out of mine affliction</sup> <sup>hell: or, the grave</sup>

For thou hadst cast<S>7993</S> me into the deep<S>4688</S>, in the midst<S>3824</S> of the seas<S>3220</S>; and the floods<S>5104</S> compassed<S>5437</S> me about: all thy billows<S>4867</S> and thy waves<S>1530</S> passed over<S>5674</S> me. <sup>midst: Heb. heart</sup>
Then I said<S>559</S>, I am cast out<S>1644</S> of thy sight<S>5869</S>; yet I will look<S>5027</S> again<S>3254</S> toward thy holy<S>6944</S> temple<S>1964</S>.

The waters<S>4325</S> compassed me about<S>661</S>, even to the soul<S>5315</S>: the depth<S>8415</S> closed me round about<S>5437</S>, the weeds<S>5488</S> were wrapped about<S>2280</S> my head<S>7218</S>.

I went down<S>3381</S> to the bottoms<S>7095</S> of the mountains<S>2022</S>; the earth<S>776</S> with her bars<S>1280</S> was about me for ever<S>5769</S>: yet hast thou brought up<S>5927</S> my life<S>2416</S> from corruption<S>7845</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S> my God<S>430</S>. <sup>bottoms: Heb. cuttings off</sup> <sup>corruption: or, the pit</sup>
When my soul<S>5315</S> fainted<S>5848</S> within me I remembered<S>2142</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>: and my prayer<S>8605</S> came in<S>935</S> unto thee, into thine holy<S>6944</S> temple<S>1964</S>.
They that observe<S>8104</S> lying<S>7723</S> vanities<S>1892</S> forsake<S>5800</S> their own mercy<S>2617</S>.

But I will sacrifice<S>2076</S> unto thee with the voice<S>6963</S> of thanksgiving<S>8426</S>; I will pay<S>7999</S> that that I have vowed<S>5087</S>. Salvation<S>3444</S> is of the LORD<S>3068</S>.
And the LORD<S>3068</S> spake<S>559</S> unto the fish<S>1709</S>, and it vomited out<S>6958</S> Jonah<S>3124</S> upon the dry<S>3004</S> land.

3.text: And the word<S>1697</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> came unto Jonah<S>3124</S> the second<S>8145</S> time, saying<S>559</S>,
Arise<S>6965</S>, go<S>3212</S> unto Nineveh<S>5210</S>, that great<S>1419</S> city<S>5892</S>, and preach<S>7121</S> unto it the preaching<S>7150</S> that I bid<S>1696</S> thee.

So Jonah<S>3124</S> arose<S>6965</S>, and went<S>3212</S> unto Nineveh<S>5210</S>, according to the word<S>1697</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>. Now Nineveh<S>5210</S> was an exceeding<S>430</S> great<S>1419</S> city<S>5892</S> of three<S>7969</S> days'<S>3117</S> journey<S>4109</S>. <sup>exceeding: Heb. of God</sup>

And Jonah<S>3124</S> began<S>2490</S> to enter<S>935</S> into the city<S>5892</S> a<S>259</S> day's<S>3117</S> journey<S>4109</S>, and he cried<S>7121</S>, and said<S>559</S>, Yet forty<S>705</S> days<S>3117</S>, and Nineveh<S>5210</S> shall be overthrown<S>2015</S>.

So the people<S>582</S> of Nineveh<S>5210</S> believed<S>539</S> God<S>430</S>, and proclaimed<S>7121</S> a fast<S>6685</S>, and put<S>3847</S> on sackcloth<S>8242</S>, from the greatest<S>1419</S> of them even to the least<S>6996</S> of them.

For word<S>1697</S> came<S>5060</S> unto the king<S>4428</S> of Nineveh<S>5210</S>, and he arose<S>6965</S> from his throne<S>3678</S>, and he laid<S>5674</S> his robe<S>155</S> from him, and covered<S>3680</S> him with sackcloth<S>8242</S>, and sat<S>3427</S> in ashes<S>665</S>.

And he caused it to be proclaimed<S>2199</S> and published<S>559</S> through Nineveh<S>5210</S> by the decree<S>2940</S> of the king<S>4428</S> and his nobles<S>1419</S>, saying<S>559</S>, Let neither man<S>120</S> nor beast<S>929</S>, herd<S>1241</S> nor flock<S>6629</S>, taste<S>2938</S> any thing<S>3972</S>: let them not feed<S>7462</S>, nor drink<S>8354</S> water<S>4325</S>: <sup>published: Heb. said</sup> <sup>nobles: Heb. great men</sup>

But let man<S>120</S> and beast<S>929</S> be covered<S>3680</S> with sackcloth<S>8242</S>, and cry<S>7121</S> mightily<S>2394</S> unto God<S>430</S>: yea, let them turn<S>7725</S> every one<S>376</S> from his evil<S>7451</S> way<S>1870</S>, and from the violence<S>2555</S> that is in their hands<S>3709</S>.
Who can tell<S>3045</S> if God<S>430</S> will turn<S>7725</S> and repent<S>5162</S>, and turn away<S>7725</S> from his fierce<S>2740</S> anger<S>639</S>, that we perish<S>6</S> not?

And God<S>430</S> saw<S>7200</S> their works<S>4639</S>, that they turned<S>7725</S> from their evil<S>7451</S> way<S>1870</S>; and God<S>430</S> repented<S>5162</S> of the evil<S>7451</S>, that he had said<S>1696</S> that he would do<S>6213</S> unto them; and he did<S>6213</S> it not.

4.text: But it displeased<S>3415</S> Jonah<S>3124</S> exceedingly<S>1419</S> <S>7451</S>, and he was very angry<S>2734</S>.

And he prayed<S>6419</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, and said<S>559</S>, I pray<S>577</S> thee, O LORD<S>3068</S>, was not this my saying<S>1697</S>, when I was yet in my country<S>127</S>? Therefore I fled<S>1272</S> before<S>6923</S> unto Tarshish<S>8659</S>: for I knew<S>3045</S> that thou art a gracious<S>2587</S> God<S>410</S>, and merciful<S>7349</S>, slow<S>750</S> to anger<S>639</S>, and of great<S>7227</S> kindness<S>2617</S>, and repentest<S>5162</S> thee of the evil<S>7451</S>.
Therefore now, O LORD<S>3068</S>, take<S>3947</S>, I beseech thee, my life<S>5315</S> from me; for it is better<S>2896</S> for me to die<S>4194</S> than to live<S>2416</S>.
Then said<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, Doest thou well<S>3190</S> to be angry<S>2734</S>? <sup>Doest: or, Art thou greatly angry?</sup>

So Jonah<S>3124</S> went out<S>3318</S> of the city<S>5892</S>, and sat<S>3427</S> on the east side<S>6924</S> of the city<S>5892</S>, and there made<S>6213</S> him a booth<S>5521</S>, and sat<S>3427</S> under it in the shadow<S>6738</S>, till he might see<S>7200</S> what would become of the city<S>5892</S>.

And the LORD<S>3068</S> God<S>430</S> prepared<S>4487</S> a gourd<S>7021</S>, and made it to come up<S>5927</S> over Jonah<S>3124</S>, that it might be a shadow<S>6738</S> over his head<S>7218</S>, to deliver<S>5337</S> him from his grief<S>7451</S>. So Jonah<S>3124</S> was exceeding<S>1419</S> <S>8057</S> glad<S>8055</S> of the gourd<S>7021</S>. <sup>gourd: or, palmcrist: Heb. Kikajon</sup> <sup>was: Heb. rejoiced with great joy</sup>
But God<S>430</S> prepared<S>4487</S> a worm<S>8438</S> when the morning<S>7837</S> rose<S>5927</S> the next day<S>4283</S>, and it smote<S>5221</S> the gourd<S>7021</S> that it withered<S>3001</S>.

And it came to pass, when the sun<S>8121</S> did arise<S>2224</S>, that God<S>430</S> prepared<S>4487</S> a vehement<S>2759</S> east<S>6921</S> wind<S>7307</S>; and the sun<S>8121</S> beat<S>5221</S> upon the head<S>7218</S> of Jonah<S>3124</S>, that he fainted<S>5968</S>, and wished<S>7592</S> in himself<S>5315</S> to die<S>4191</S>, and said<S>559</S>, It is better<S>2896</S> for me to die<S>4194</S> than to live<S>2416</S>. <sup>vehement: or, silent</sup>

And God<S>430</S> said<S>559</S> to Jonah<S>3124</S>, Doest thou well<S>3190</S> to be angry<S>2734</S> for the gourd<S>7021</S>? And he said<S>559</S>, I do well<S>3190</S> to be angry<S>2734</S>, even unto death<S>4194</S>. <sup>Doest: or, Art thou greatly angry?</sup> <sup>I do well: or, I am greatly angry</sup>

Then said<S>559</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, Thou hast had pity<S>2347</S> on the gourd<S>7021</S>, for the which thou hast not laboured<S>5998</S>, neither madest it grow<S>1431</S>; which came up in a night<S>1121</S> <S>3915</S>, and perished<S>6</S> in a night<S>1121</S> <S>3915</S>: <sup>had pity: or, spared</sup> <sup>came: Heb. was the son of the night</sup>

And should not I spare<S>2347</S> Nineveh<S>5210</S>, that great<S>1419</S> city<S>5892</S>, wherein are<S>3426</S> more<S>7235</S> than sixscore<S>8147</S> <S>6240</S> thousand<S>7239</S> persons<S>120</S> that cannot discern<S>3045</S> between their right hand<S>3225</S> and their left hand<S>8040</S>; and also much<S>7227</S> cattle<S>929</S>?
